conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
I'll be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I need to ask you a few questions.

Let me start with the first question:

**Do you have any children, and if so, how many children are you responsible for?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I do have children. I have two children - Alex who is 8 years old, and Blake who is 9 years old. They both live at home with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. It's helpful to know you have two children, Alex (8 years old) and Blake (9 years old), both living at home with you.

Now I need to ask:

**Are you currently living in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I am living in the UK.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great, thank you. Now I need to understand your responsibility for the children:

**Are you the person responsible for Alex and Blake? This means you're the main person looking after them and responsible for decisions about their upbringing, food, clothing, and general care.**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I am. I'm the one who looks after them both - I take care of their daily needs, make decisions about their schooling, buy their clothes and food, all of that. They live with me full-time.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for confirming that. Now I need to ask about any care arrangements:

**Are either Alex or Blake currently in hospital or in the care of local authorities (such as in foster care or a children's home)?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, neither of them are. They're both at home with me full-time.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. Now I need to ask about any other people who might be involved:

**Does anyone else claim Child Benefit for Alex or Blake, or is there another person who also considers themselves responsible for either of them?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else claims Child Benefit for them. It's just me looking after them and I'm the only one responsible for them.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing all that information. I now have enough details to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit.

Let me review your situation:

- You have two children: Alex (8 years old) and Blake (9 years old)
- Both children live with you full-time in the UK
- You are responsible for both children and provide their daily care
- Neither child is in hospital or in local authority care
- No one else claims Child Benefit for either child

Based on the eligibility criteria, you meet all the requirements for Child Benefit for both children.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Good news! You are eligible for Child Benefit for both Alex and Blake.**

You meet all the necessary requirements:
- You're living in the UK
- Both children are under 16
- You're responsible for them and they live with you
- Neither child is in hospital or care
- No one else is claiming Child Benefit for them

You can proceed with applying for Child Benefit for both children. You'll receive payments for both of them, though please note that you may need to consider the High Income Child Benefit Charge if you or your partner earn over £50,000 per year (but this doesn't affect your eligibility to claim).

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 2 children:
  - Alex is 8 years old and lives with you.
  - Blake is 9 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Blake:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 9 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex is 8 years old (under 16), lives with the claimant in the UK full-time, the claimant is responsible for Alex's care and upbringing, Alex is not in hospital or local authority care, and no one else claims Child Benefit for Alex. All eligibility criteria are met.

Blake is 9 years old (under 16), lives with the claimant in the UK full-time, the claimant is responsible for Blake's care and upbringing, Blake is not in hospital or local authority care, and no one else claims Child Benefit for Blake. All eligibility criteria are met.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for both children. They are living in the UK, are responsible for two children (Alex, 8, and Blake, 9) who both live with them full-time, neither child is in hospital or care, and no one else claims Child Benefit for either child. All eligibility requirements under UK Child Benefit rules are satisfied.
